mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending earnings downgrades, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in real retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series shows negative growth, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during inflationary supply bottlenecks, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of expansion periods when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 198.0, persistent inflationary pressures force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >55% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power when real GDP growth remains above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and higher aggregate consumer spending, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or payroll growth fails to predict positive returns when accompanied by rising unit labor costs.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based demand acceleration, prompting institutional portfolio rebalancing into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns when inventory adjustments offset demand growth, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals manufacturing contraction and weakening business investment, which triggers earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of contraction periods, or the signal fails to predict returns when the decline is driven by temporary weather-related disruptions rather than structural demand weakness.
